authoritative_sources.title: 19 CFR Part 141, Entry of Merchandise
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Homeland Security, U.S. Customs and Border Protection
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary U.S. customs-entry regulation for required entry information, invoice evidence, and entry-related workflow dependencies.

authoritative_sources.title: 19 CFR Part 142, Entry Process
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Homeland Security, U.S. Customs and Border Protection
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative CBP workflow rule for formal-entry process timing, release-adjacent process steps, and procedural dependencies.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Filing and progression through formal-entry states

authoritative_sources.title: ACE ABI CATAIR Entry Summary Create/Update
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CBP's operational system reference for ACE entry-summary data requirements, message handling, and response semantics.

authoritative_sources.title: FinCEN Advisory on Trade-Based Money Laundering
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury, Financial Crimes Enforcement Network
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Treasury AML guidance for suspicious trade patterns that can change whether a customs-entry workflow may safely proceed.
used_for: suspicious value or document pattern handling

authoritative_sources.title: Sanctions Programs and Country Information
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury, Office of Foreign Assets Control
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary sanctions authority baseline for country and party restrictions that can block customs-entry execution or require compliance escalation.

authoritative_sources.title: Trade Based Money Laundering
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized international risk-control framework for TBML control patterns and escalation design in trade workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Risk-control design for suspicious trade activity

authoritative_sources.title: WCO Data Model
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized customs data standard for harmonized data elements and consistent field-level evidence handling across customs systems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Artifact and field normalization across systems

authoritative_sources.title: Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official U.S. tariff-classification source for customs commodity terminology and classification evidence references.

workflow_stages.name: Accepted intake and prerequisite verification
workflow_stages.description: Validate importer-of-record, broker authority, shipment context, commercial documents, and jurisdictional prerequisites before any filing recommendation.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Entry packet assembly and data normalization
workflow_stages.description: Normalize classification, valuation, quantities, origin, and customs data fields across document and system sources before submission or update.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Filing, response handling, and state progression
workflow_stages.description: Move work through queued, filed, accepted, rejected, held, rework, released, or completed states based on ACE or broker-system evidence and authoritative rules.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Exception, rework, and discrepancy management
workflow_stages.description: Handle rejects, missing documents, mismatched data, and valuation or classification disagreements without silently skipping required process steps.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Fraud, AML, sanctions, and financial-impact escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when suspicious trade patterns, sanctions risks, or duty and payment impacts introduce regulated-risk or consumer-impact concerns outside the lane.

workflow_stages.name: Completion confirmation and evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Produce disposition and completion records only when filing outcome, release or rejection disposition, and required evidence references are present.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

artifacts: Commercial invoice, packing list, bill of lading or airway bill, and arrival notice
CBP entry and entry-summary artifacts, including 3461 and 7501-equivalent data where applicable
Importer-of-record, bond, power-of-attorney, and broker instruction records
Commodity classification candidates, HTS references, origin data, and valuation basis records
ACE or broker-system messages, status responses, reject details, and document imaging references
Duty, fee, payment, reconciliation, and communication-log artifacts
KYC, AML, sanctions, fraud, approval-history, and escalation records
systems_of_record: Customs broker operating systems and case queues
ERP, order management, and shipment source systems
General ledger, duty, and payment tracking systems
Sanctions, fraud, AML, and compliance monitoring systems

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, blocked, or out of scope based on customs-entry workflow ownership.
Validate minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context required for safe entry progression.
Prepare execution status updates, exception notes, trade execution summaries, and completion evidence records tied to named artifacts.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete work based on documented lifecycle state and evidence.
escalate: Missing or contradictory entry documents, ACE statuses, or tenant policies materially change the outcome.
Classification finality, valuation disputes, or customs-law interpretation is required.
Fraud, AML, sanctions, or consumer-impact conditions change whether the work may safely proceed.
Disclosure, payment, or reconciliation conflicts affect duties, fees, or downstream customer impact.
refuse: Requests to fabricate approvals, regulated advice, broker authority, or completion evidence.
Requests that require legal, licensed, policy-override, or duty/payment approval authority outside the lane.
Requests to mark work complete while filing disposition, release evidence, or reject-resolution evidence is missing.

domain_failure_modes: Filing or advancing an entry without the minimum document set or importer-of-record truth.
Treating unresolved ACE rejects or message mismatches as completed work.
Allowing ambiguous classification or valuation evidence to silently drive progression instead of escalation.
Ignoring TBML or sanctions indicators embedded in invoice, party, or geography data.
Closing a case without traceable evidence of filing disposition, release, reject resolution, or approved closure.
Applying public customs defaults when tenant policy requires stricter document imaging, approvals, or jurisdictional routing.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Disclosure or reconciliation conflict that requires escalation
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific definition of acceptable release evidence when ACE and broker-platform states lag each other
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require tenant precedence rules and block completion until the designated system-of-record state is confirmed.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Whether tenant policy requires customs-entry specialists to stop before any post-release duty reconciliation step
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Preserve the public baseline and route to duty or drawback owners when tenant policy extends or narrows the lane.
